title: potentilla adscendens f. minoriflora
intro: Potentilla adscendens f. minoriflora, also known as the dwarf potentilla or small-flowered cinquefoil, is a low-growing perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Asia. It is a member of the rose family and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating respiratory issues, due to its high content of tannins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. Potentilla adscendens f. minoriflora can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote overall well-being.
